title: Sida littoralis
uses: Sida littoralis is known for its potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine. It has been used to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism. In some regions, the plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties and is used to alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Additionally, Sida littoralis has been used as a treatment for skin conditions and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
taxonomy: Sida littoralis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the hollyhock or mallow family. It belongs to the genus Sida, which comprises about 30 species of annual or perennial herbs and shrubs. Sida littoralis is native to Australia and is often found in coastal areas and wetlands. The species is also known as sea sida or coastal sida. It is often included in the tribe Malveae, which is part of the larger Malvaceae family.
benefits: Sida littoralis is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, and research suggests that it may have several health benefits. Sida littoralis has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat conditions such as fever, headache, and skin infections.
